title: QueryForge
description: A complete relational database engine — lexer, Pratt parser, a statistics-driven cost-based optimizer with cost-based join reordering (Selinger-style subset DP), index-only (covering) scans, bitmap-AND of multiple indexes and composite B+Tree indexes, a Volcano-model executor with hash & sort-merge joins, an external merge sort, GROUPING SETS/ROLLUP/CUBE with GROUPING(), ordered-set aggregates (PERCENTILE_CONT/DISC, MODE), correlated subqueries, CTEs (incl. WITH RECURSIVE), set operations, standard-grade window functions, statistical aggregates with FILTER, transactions, CSV import, an SVG chart view and an EXPLAIN visualizer. First-class temporal types — DATE, TIME, TIMESTAMP and INTERVAL as tagged values that index, sort, group and join like any column — with typed literals, calendar-aware date arithmetic (month-clamping), EXTRACT/DATE_TRUNC/AGE, MAKE_* constructors and CURRENT_DATE/TIMESTAMP. Full declarative integrity: PRIMARY KEY/UNIQUE (composite), CHECK and DEFAULT, and FOREIGN KEYs with ON DELETE/UPDATE referential actions (CASCADE/RESTRICT/SET NULL/SET DEFAULT, recursively), ALTER TABLE schema evolution, and atomic statements that roll back wholesale on any constraint failure. And first-class exact numerics: arbitrary-precision DECIMAL/NUMERIC (BigInt-backed) where 0.1 + 0.2 is exactly 0.3 and money SUMs never lose a cent — DECIMAL(p,s) columns, exact arithmetic & aggregates, and Postgres-style TO_CHAR currency formatting. Plus VIEWs (CREATE [OR REPLACE] VIEW, inlined wherever they're used, even inside other views), UPSERT (INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING / DO UPDATE with EXCLUDED), and an optimizer that decorrelates a correlated [NOT] EXISTS into a single hash SemiJoin / AntiJoin instead of re-running the subquery per row. And now first-class JSON (jsonb-style): a tagged, normalized value (sorted/de-duplicated keys) that indexes, sorts, groups, joins and persists like any column, with the path/containment operators (-> ->> #> #>> @> <@ ?), a Postgres ::TYPE postfix cast, a scalar/aggregate library (JSON_BUILD_OBJECT/ARRAY, JSON_EXTRACT_PATH, JSONB_SET, JSON_PRETTY, JSON_AGG, JSON_OBJECT_AGG, …) and set-returning table functions in FROM (JSON_ARRAY_ELEMENTS, JSON_EACH, …) that unnest JSON into rows. And now first-class FULL-TEXT SEARCH (Postgres tsvector/tsquery): two more tagged values threaded through the same value plumbing so a search document indexes, sorts, groups, joins and persists like any column — built on a from-scratch Porter (1980) stemmer, an English stop-word list and a positional tokenizer. The @@ match operator does boolean (& | !), prefix (:*), weight-filtered (:A) and true positional PHRASE search (a <-> b, a <N> b) via a position-set executor; to_tsvector / to_tsquery / plainto_tsquery / phraseto_tsquery / websearch_to_tsquery build documents and queries; ts_rank / ts_rank_cd rank by A/B/C/D weights and cover density; ts_headline highlights; setweight / strip / || shape vectors. The capstone is a real GIN inverted index (CREATE INDEX … USING GIN) — maintained on every insert/update/delete and across snapshots — that the planner turns into a posting-list candidate probe plus an exact recheck (a GinScan in EXPLAIN), so search is sublinear yet byte-for-byte identical to the sequential-scan filter. And now STANDARD-GRADE WINDOW FUNCTIONS: a from-scratch frame engine with all three modes — ROWS (physical), RANGE (typed value offsets over numbers, exact DECIMAL and DATE/TIMESTAMP ± INTERVAL, honouring ASC/DESC) and GROUPS (peer groups) — each with the EXCLUDE clause (NO OTHERS / CURRENT ROW / GROUP / TIES), aggregate FILTER (WHERE …) in the frame, IGNORE NULLS / RESPECT NULLS value functions (FIRST_VALUE/LAST_VALUE/NTH_VALUE/LAG/LEAD), the statistical (STDDEV/VARIANCE) and ordered-set (PERCENTILE_CONT/DISC, MODE) families as windows, and a named WINDOW clause with reference inheritance (OVER (w …)) — all proven against the GROUP BY aggregates in 285 in-app self-tests. Built from scratch in TypeScript, running entirely in the browser.
